data.title: Robinhood Chain Tops Ethereum Ecosystem Revenue With 44% Daily Increase
data.description:
Robinhood Chain ranked first in Ethereum ecosystem chain revenue over the past 24 hours, according to growthepie data. According to Foresight News, Robinhood Chain generated $361,000, followed by Ethereum mainnet at $273,000, Polygon PoS at $86,800, Base Chain at $62,700, and Arbitrum One at $16,400.

Robinhood Chain rose 44% day over day, placing it first among Ethereum ecosystem chains.

data.title: UBS Analyst Says China AI Models Have Cost Edge
data.description:
UBS Securities China internet analyst Xiong Wei said Chinese artificial intelligence models have their strongest global market advantage in lower training and inference costs, according to Ming Pao.

She said the edge does not depend on price subsidies or vendors cutting margins, but instead comes from underlying innovation in technology and architecture.

data.title: HKTDC Restructures Operations Around Six Industry Clusters
data.description:
The Hong Kong Trade Development Council has restructured its operations and global office resources as it marks its 60th anniversary, according to Ming Pao.

Chairman Vincent H.C. Ma said the changes, effective July 1, will center on a “cluster approach” that reallocates functions into six major industry groups to improve operating efficiency and support companies in planning broader promotion strategies. He added that the number of vice presidents has been expanded to six from two.

HKTDC Executive Director Sophia Chong said the adjustment is a “delayering” exercise that flattens the organization from three layers, namely executive director, vice president and assistant director, to two layers, executive director and vice president, while also retitling posts.

data.title: InnoLight's Hong Kong IPO Draws 7 Times Retail Oversubscription
data.description:
InnoLight Technology (3308), the ninth-largest new listing by fundraising amount in Hong Kong stock market history, will close its share offer next Monday, July 27. As of 6:30 PM today, the retail tranche had been 7 times oversubscribed on margin, with brokers lending HK$44.228 billion in margin financing, according to Ming Pao.

Futu Securities led the field with HK$19.638 billion in loans.

data.title: STOCKS | Citigroup Cuts IBM Price Target to $245 From $255
data.description: According to Jin10, Citigroup lowered its price target for IBM (IBM.N) to $245 from $255.

data.title: NHTSA Starts Rulemaking on Vehicle Door Emergency Escape Systems
data.description:
According to Lianhe Zaobao citing Bloomberg, the U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said on Thursday, July 23, that it will begin the process of drafting new federal rules to ensure drivers and passengers can safely exit a vehicle after an accident. The agency also approved a petition calling for a requirement that all cars be equipped with reliable, easy-to-use door escape systems. If adopted, the new rules would apply to all new cars sold in the United States.

The move follows a series of accidents in which occupants were trapped inside vehicles and, in some cases, died. Hidden, electronically controlled door handles used by Tesla and other automakers have been linked to several such incidents in recent years. In some cases, Tesla passengers survived high-speed crashes but later died or were seriously injured in fires because they could not escape the vehicle.

A Silicon Valley engineer filed the petition with NHTSA in November last year, seeking new federal safety standards. NHTSA said it will gather more information and seek public comment to determine whether starting a rulemaking process under the Vehicle Safety Act is appropriate. Brooks, executive director of the U.S. Auto Safety Center, said the move is an important first step in improving electric door and escape safety. The process could take years, and even if it proceeds smoothly, manufacturers may not have to comply with final rules until after 2030. Illinois Democratic Representative Kelly also introduced a bill earlier this year that would require new cars to have manual door-opening devices and ensure emergency responders can enter vehicles if power is cut.

data.title: Corporate Bitcoin Treasuries Could Face Up to 128,500 BTC in Calendar-Driven Selling
data.description:
Corporate Bitcoin treasuries could face calendar-driven selling of 77,100–128,500 BTC in a bear case over the next two years. According to NS3.AI, Strategy reported 843,738 BTC as of May 25, along with $6.7 billion in convertible notes and $15.5 billion in preferred stock.

The stress case estimates potential selling could exceed 192,800 BTC if one large treasury company loses access to refinancing.

data.title: World Bank Sees Morocco Inflation Rising to 2.4% in 2026
data.description: According to Jin10, the World Bank said Morocco's inflation will rise to 2.4% in 2026 because of the Iran war.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards: U.S. Fifth Fleet Observation Tower in Bahrain Hit
data.description: According to Jin10, Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they struck a U.S. Fifth Fleet observation tower in Bahrain.

data.title: U.S. Secretary Rubio: We Should Have Struck Iran Earlier
data.description: According to Jin10, U.S. Secretary of State Rubio said, "We should have struck Iran earlier."

data.title: Congo Ebola Cases Near 3,000 After Data Review
data.description:
Ebola infections in the Democratic Republic of Congo have risen to almost 3,000 confirmed cases, with more than 1,250 deaths, after health authorities reconciled provincial and national databases, according to Bloomberg.

Officials also reported dozens of new cases in the virus's epicenter.

data.title: STOCKS | India State Refiners Seek Crude as Middle East Flows Stall
data.description:
State-owned refiners in India are seeking alternative crude supplies as they worry cargoes booked from Saudi Arabia and Iraq may not arrive on time, according to Bloomberg.

The companies are trying to avoid disruptions to feedstock deliveries as Middle East flows are choked.

data.title: Bitcoin Profitability Improves, But Analysts See Risk of Another Pullback
data.description:
Bitcoin market profitability is improving, but on-chain data still do not confirm the start of a new bull market, and the risk of another decline remains. According to ChainCatcher, CryptoQuant data show Bitcoin's Supply in Profit has risen to 57.5%, up from a June 30, 2026 low of 46.2% and close to 60%.

However, analysts said the recovery in profitable supply still needs sustained confirmation. Historical cycles suggest a bear market is only considered over when two conditions are met: the 30-day simple moving average of Long-Term Holder SOPR stays above 1 for several weeks, and Bitcoin's Supply in Profit remains steadily above 64%.

Analysts noted that this cycle has already seen one false breakout. From April 28 to June 1, the LTH-SOPR average stayed above 1 for 35 straight days, while Supply in Profit briefly reached 67%, before the market pulled back again. At present, the 30-day SMA for long-term holders has remained below 1 for more than 50 days, which is still viewed as an important risk signal for the strength of the recovery.

data.title: STOCKS | Goldman Sachs Raises TOPIX 12-Month Target to 4,500 From 4,400
data.description: According to Jin10, Goldman Sachs raised its 12-month target for Japan's TOPIX index to 4,500 points from 4,400 points.

data.title: Apple Reportedly Seeks 20% Cut in iPhone 18 Pro Max OLED Panel Price
data.description: Apple is pressuring upstream suppliers to sharply lower OLED panel prices as rising memory chip costs push up iPhone overall costs. According to Jin10, foreign media reported that Apple has asked panel suppliers to price the OLED panels used in the iPhone 18 Pro Max at about $70, down roughly 20% from the previous model. Industry sources estimated that panels currently supplied by Samsung Display and LG Display cost an average of $66.5, even below Apple's target price.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Dollar Heads for Best Week in a Month as Haven Demand Builds
data.description:
The dollar is heading for its best week in a month as investors return to the traditional haven amid heightened geopolitical tensions, according to Bloomberg.

The move reflects renewed demand for safe-haven assets as markets react to the latest geopolitical backdrop.

data.title: Euronext Considers Private Securities Market to Challenge London
data.description: Euronext NV is considering creating a market for private securities, a move that could intensify competition with London Stock Exchange Group Plc as exchanges seek to give investors access to fast-growing private companies, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: ECB Still More Likely to Hike Than Hold, Simkus Says
data.description:
European Central Bank Governing Council member Gediminas Simkus said the ECB is still more likely to raise borrowing costs than keep them unchanged, according to Bloomberg.

His comments suggest policymakers have not ruled out another rate increase even as they weigh the outlook for inflation and growth.

data.title: Economists Keep Fed Cut Call as Markets Price in Hikes
data.description: Economists still expect the Federal Reserve’s next move to be an interest-rate cut, even as markets price in higher borrowing costs by year-end, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: EU Escalates TikTok Probe Over Teen Privacy Protections
data.description:
The European Union escalated its probe into TikTok under the bloc’s content moderation rulebook, accusing the social network of failing to protect teen users’ safety and privacy, according to Bloomberg.

The case adds to regulatory pressure on the ByteDance-owned app as EU authorities examine whether it has met platform safety obligations.
